justification: All pass criteria met: Regional isolation model designed with EU/US/APAC as autonomous units with no raw data crossing regions. GDPR and CCPA data residency compliance addressed architecturally (PII never leaves region, enforced at network/schema/application layers). Global dashboard designed to consume only anonymized/aggregated metrics via Global Event Bridge — no cross-region raw data access. Courier assignment regional isolation correctly analyzed with belt-and-suspenders enforcement (Delivery Engine scoped to region + CourierAssignment aggregate guard). Trade-offs explicitly include 3-region operational overhead ('managing 3× the infrastructure' risk rated High). Degraded EU service during outage designed via multi-AZ within region approach with acceptance that full regional failure means degraded service.

observations.text: Defense-in-depth approach to data residency: schema validation on event bridge, network isolation, application-level enforcement — not just policy
Phased rollout plan (EU refactor → Global Layer → US → APAC) is pragmatic and de-risks migration
Explicitly calls out APAC data residency complexity (APPI, PDPA, Australia Privacy Act) as a low-confidence area needing legal review
Global dashboard eventual consistency explicitly acknowledged with 'as of N seconds ago' watermark design
Pitfalls section directly addresses the most common failure modes (cross-region DB links, global customer ID, sync cross-region calls)
Schema registry with CI enforcement prevents accidental PII leakage to global layer
Degraded EU service specifics are acknowledged as low-confidence — what exactly customers see during outage (cached order history, inability to place new orders) could be more concrete
Schema migration coordination across 3 regions not explicitly addressed as a risk — only deployment consistency is mentioned via GitOps

agent_output_excerpt:
## Executive Summary

The multi-region logistics platform requires a **federated architecture with regional sovereignty** — each region (EU, US, APAC) operates as an autonomous unit owning its customer and operational data, while a lightweight global coordination layer aggregates metrics and manages cross-region concerns.
